headline: Nike (NKE) Beat On Q4 Earnings, Is The Turnaround Fully Priced?
summary: Nike (NKE) reported fourth quarter earnings that came in ahead of expectations, helped by a sizeable tariff refund. However, a sharp sales decline in Greater China and guidance for continued sales pressure kept investor sentiment cautious. See our latest analysis for NIKE. Nike’s recent Q4 beat and tariff refund have not stopped the share price from falling sharply year to date, with the year to date share price return down 31.72% and the 1 year total shareholder return down 39.94%. This...

headline: How NIKE’s (NKE) Capital Returns Show the Tax Tradeoff Between Dividends and Buybacks
summary: NIKE, Inc. (NYSE:NKE) is one of the dividend stocks picked by financial media as investors ask whether dividend stocks are tax-efficient. On June 30, Nike reported fiscal 2026 fourth-quarter and full-year results and said it returned about $609 million to shareholders through dividends in the fourth quarter, up 3% from the prior year. headline: Kohl’s prices thousands of back-to-school items under $25 as consumers look for deals
summary: The season could be a critical test for the struggling department store, whose years-long turnaround has failed to gain much traction.

headline: Back-to-School Confidence Starts at Kohl’s With Trusted Brands, Fresh Styles, and Thousands of Products Under $25
summary: MENOMONEE FALLS, Wis., July 08, 2026--This back-to-school season, Kohl’s (NYSE: KSS) is making shopping easier by focusing on the brands, styles, and sizes families need to start the school year off right. From national favorites like Nike and Levi’s to By Kohl’s brands such as SO, Tek Gear, FLX, and Jumping Beans, Kohl’s combines the trends kids want with the quality and affordability parents expect, including thousands of products under $25.

headline: Nike expects nearly $1B in IEEPA tariff refunds
summary: The sportswear brand has collected more than $300 million in refunds for the invalidated levies, as of the quarter ending May 31.

headline: WHOOP Names Dirk-Jan "DJ" van Hameren Chief Marketing Officer as Member Base Surpasses 3 Million
summary: BOSTON, July 08, 2026--WHOOP, the human performance company, today named Dirk-Jan "DJ" van Hameren as Chief Marketing Officer. The appointment comes as WHOOP surpasses 3 million members worldwide after adding its latest one million members in just seven months. The former Nike Global Chief Marketing Officer and two-time Olympian joins WHOOP to help build the world's defining global brand for human performance and proactive health.
